title: strobilanthes urens
intro: Strobilanthes urens, a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, is commonly known as the 'stinking rosemallow' due to its unpleasant odor. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Strobilanthes urens include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections, as well as its ability to act as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
